title: Defense Budget and Acquisition: Research & Analysis | CSIS
text:
Defense Budget and Acquisition

CSIS experts track the foremost budget issues in the defense policy sphere, exploring trends in the overall defense budget, military readiness, force structure, defense acquisition, and military compensation in a broader effort to assess the alignment of the country’s defense strategy and its resources.

Image

Photo: MPI/Getty Images

Solid Rocket Motors for Missile Defense: Challenges and Opportunities for Expanding the Industrial Base

Air and missile defense interceptors play a critical role in modern conflict. Accelerating production of solid rocket motors and strengthening the munitions industrial base will require an understanding of the market and both the challenges and opportunities for expansion.

Report
by

Wes Rumbaugh
and
Tom Karako

2026 Global Security Forum, America at 250: A Defining Moment for American Statecraft and Military Power

CSIS' flagship Defense and Security Forum will convene leaders from government, industry, finance, and academia to discuss how American statecraft and military power can drive strategic advantage in the years ahead.

Event

— June 30, 2026

Is the Industrial Base on a Wartime Footing? A Progress Report

On this episode of All About the Base, Dr. Jerry McGinn assesses where progress toward placing the industrial base on a wartime footing is being made across priority areas like munitions, supply chains, and allied cooperation, and identifies where greater focus is needed.

Event

— June 29, 2026

Innovation to Deployment: Fixing the Pentagon’s Acquisition Gap

On this episode of All About the Base, Dr. Jerry McGinn joins the Cache Me If You Can podcast with host Lauryn Williams to discuss the progress and implications of the Pentagon's efforts to reform defense acquisition and field emerging technologies at speed and scale.

Event

— June 15, 2026

Solid Rocket Motors for Missile Defense: Challenges and Opportunities for Expanding the Industrial Base

Air and missile defense interceptors play a critical role in modern conflict. Accelerating production of solid rocket motors and strengthening the munitions industrial base will require an understanding of the market and both the challenges and opportunities for expansion.

Report
by

Wes Rumbaugh
and
Tom Karako

— June 12, 2026

Accelerating Warfighting Capabilities: Putting the Industrial Base on a Wartime Footing

On this episode of "All About the Base" Dr. Jerry McGinn moderates a panel of Pentagon leaders at the Naval Postgraduate School’s Annual Acquisition Research Symposium & Innovation Summit on efforts to place the industrial base on a wartime footing.

Event

— June 1, 2026

Rebuilding U.S. Missile Inventory: A Multiyear Project

High expenditure of key munitions in Operation Epic Fury has created a window of vulnerability until inventories return to pre-war levels and then reach levels that war planners desire.

Commentary
by

Mark F. Cancian
and
Chris H. Park

— May 27, 2026

Testing the Future of Joint All-Domain Operations in Africa

Africa’s open terrain could become the proving ground for next-generation warfare, helping the U.S. and partners test drones, electronic warfare, and joint all-domain operations while signaling deterrence to China and Russia.

Commentary
by

Benjamin Jensen
and
Soufiane Ammagui

— May 26, 2026

Revisiting the Relationship Between Economic and Sea Power

Sea power begins with economic power. MAP and the SHIPS Act seek to rebuild the maritime industrial base—shipyards, mariners, logistics networks, and commercial fleets—that underpins U.S. naval strategy and will bolster U.S. maritime competitiveness against China.

Commentary
by

Benjamin Jensen
and
Mackenzie Eaglen

— May 22, 2026

Can Trusted Partners Help Secure U.S. Drone Supremacy?

Please join the Aerospace Security Project for a discussion on trusted drone manufacturing, resilient supply chains, and the future of defense industrial cooperation.

Event

— May 14, 2026

U.S. Missile Inventory Check: A Window of Vulnerability?

Mark F. Cancian and Chris H. Park discuss the "Last Rounds?" report and assess whether the U.S. military nears the point of going "Winchester"— or running out of ammunition.

Event

— May 1, 2026
